name: Wagon Wheel — Play Along
description: Play through 'Wagon Wheel' at actual song length and tempo. Sections are timed to match the real arrangement — hit play and strum along from intro to outro.

steps.name: Intro
steps.instructions: Strum the 8-bar progression to set the feel: G D Em C | G D C C.
steps.notes: One pass through the progression. Establish the groove.

steps.name: Verse 1
steps.instructions: 'Headed down south to the land of the pines...' Two passes through the progression.
steps.notes: Sing while strumming. The vocal rhythm is independent of the strum pattern.

steps.name: Chorus 1
steps.instructions: 'So rock me mama like a wagon wheel...' Push the energy up. Two passes.
steps.notes: Dig into the strums — this is the big sing-along.

steps.name: Verse 2
steps.instructions: 'Running from the cold up in New England...' Same progression, same energy as verse 1.

steps.name: Chorus 2
steps.instructions: 'So rock me mama like a wagon wheel...' Same chorus — belt it out.

steps.name: Bridge / Solo
steps.instructions: Fiddle solo over the same changes. Keep the rhythm tight and steady.
steps.notes: You're the rhythm section here — lock in the groove.

steps.name: Verse 3
steps.instructions: 'Walkin' to the south out of Roanoke...' Last verse — bring it home.

steps.name: Final Chorus
steps.instructions: 'So rock me mama like a wagon wheel...' Last chorus — give it everything.
steps.notes: Big finish energy. Strum with conviction.

steps.name: Outro
steps.instructions: One last pass. Let the strumming taper off and end with a strong G.
steps.notes: Let the final G ring out.
